name: java-and-memory
description: How to fix Java-version and memory problems — wrong Java for the Minecraft version, downloading the right runtime, setting instance memory, OutOfMemory crashes. Use when the user mentions Java or memory ("java不对","内存","爆内存","OutOfMemory").
body:
# Java & memory

Goal: the right Java for the game version, and a sane memory setting — together these are the
#1 "game won't even start" cause after mods.

## Route
- Wrong/missing Java runtime, picking or downloading a version → `java-runtime`.
- Memory allocation, OutOfMemory crashes → `set-memory`.

## Diagnose first
A wrong-Java failure shows up as an immediate crash with `UnsupportedClassVersionError` or a
launcher warning. An OOM crash shows `OutOfMemoryError` in the log. If it's not obvious which
one you're looking at, `diagnose-crash` handles reading the log — this skill only fixes the
cause once it's identified, it doesn't read logs itself.

## Don't
- Don't guess between Java-version and memory as the cause — check the log via `diagnose-crash`
  when unsure.
- Don't keep raising memory as a blanket fix — repeated OOM despite generous memory means a mod
  is leaking, not that more RAM is needed.
